perturbation.original_text: pursuant to an agency agreement (the "Agency Agreement") dated as of May 22, 2015 between the Corporation and the agents named therein, [ ] (the "Agent") and [ ] (the "U.S. Affiliate"), the U.S. registered broker-dealer affiliate of the Agent, hereby certify as follows: (i) on the date hereof and on the date of each offer of Securities by us in the United States and each subsequent sale to such offerees by the Corporation, the U.S. Affiliate is and was: (A) a duly registered as a broker-dealer under the U.S. Exchange Act and duly registered as a broker-dealer under the laws of each state where it made offers of Securities (unless exempt from such registration requirements); and (B) a member of and in good standing with the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority, Inc.;
perturbation.changed_text: pursuant to an agency agreement (the "Service Contract") dated as of May 22, 2015 between the Corporation and the agents named therein, [ ] (the "Representative") and [ ] (the "U.S. Subsidiary"), the U.S. registered broker-dealer affiliate of the Agent, hereby certify as follows: (i) on the date hereof and on the date of each offer of Securities by us in the United States and each subsequent sale to such offerees by the Corporation, the U.S. Subsidiary is and was: (A) a duly registered as a broker-dealer under the U.S. Exchange Act and duly registered as a broker-dealer under the laws of each state where it made offers of Securities (unless exempt from such registration requirements); and (B) a member of and in good standing with the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority, Inc.;
perturbation.explanation: The terms "Agency Agreement" are changed to "Service Contract", "Agent" to "Representative", and "U.S. Affiliate" to "U.S. Subsidiary". This introduces uncertainty. Later in the document, obligations and representations are tied to the "Agent" and "Agency Agreement". The term 'Agent' is still used in the text 'affiliate of the Agent' even after its definition was 'Representative', creating an in-text contradiction and leaving open to interpretation as to whether "Service Contract" now governs those relationships and responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (vi) the Agent represents and warrants that with respect to Offered Shares to be sold in reliance on Rule 506(b) of Regulation D, none of it, the U.S. Affiliate, any of its or the U.S. Affiliate's directors, executive officers, general partners, managing members or other officers participating in the Offering, or any other person associated with the Agent who will receive, directly or indirectly, remuneration for solicitation of Subscribers of Offered Shares pursuant to Rule 506(b) of Regulation D (each, a "Dealer Covered Person" and, together, "Dealer Covered Persons"), is subject to any Disqualification Event except for a Disqualification Event (i) covered by Rule 506(d)(2)(i) of Regulation D and (ii) a description of which has been furnished in writing to the Corporation prior to the date hereof;
perturbation.justification: The change from "Agency Agreement" to "Service Contract", "Agent" to "Representative", and "U.S. Affiliate" to "U.S. Subsidiary" introduces a clear contradiction. The contradicted text refers to the "Agent" and the "U.S. Affiliate" and imposes obligations and representations on them. The changed text redefines these terms, but still uses 'affiliate of the Agent', thereby creating ambiguity and a direct conflict regarding which entity is bound by these obligations. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the contract uses multiple terms (Agent/Representative, Agency Agreement/Service Contract, US Affiliate/US Subsidiary) without clearly defining them in relation to the contradicted clause, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: immediately prior to offering the Securities to persons in the United States, we had reasonable grounds to believe and did believe that each such person was an Accredited Investor, based upon a pre-existing relationship, and, on the date hereof, we continue to believe that each such offeree purchasing Securities from the Corporation is an Accredited Investor; (iv) no form of Directed Selling Efforts, General Solicitation or General Advertising was used by us in connection with the offer of the Securities by us in the United States, and we have not acted in any manner involving public offering within the meaning of Section 4(a) (2) of the U.S. Securities Act in connection with the offer of the Securities by us in the United States;
perturbation.changed_text: immediately prior to offering the Shares to persons in the United States, we had reasonable grounds to believe and did believe that each such person was an Accredited Investor, based upon a pre-existing relationship, and, on the date hereof, we continue to believe that each such offeree purchasing Securities from the Corporation is an Accredited Investor; (iv) no form of Directed Selling Efforts, General Solicitation or General Advertising was used by us in connection with the offer of the Shares by us in the United States, and we have not acted in any manner involving private offering within the meaning of Section 4(a) (2) of the U.S. Securities Act in connection with the offer of the Securities by us in the United States;
perturbation.explanation: Here the term "Securities" is replaced with "Shares" in subsection (iii) and (iv), but the other mentions of Securities remain unchanged in subsection (iv). This creates an in-text contradiction in subsection (iv), as it's unclear if the prohibition of 'public offering' applies to both 'Shares' and 'Securities' or only the 'Securities'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (iv) no form of Directed Selling Efforts, General Solicitation or General Advertising was used by us in connection with the offer of the Shares by us in the United States, and we have not acted in any manner involving private offering within the meaning of Section 4(a) (2) of the U.S. Securities Act in connection with the offer of the Securities by us in the United States;
perturbation.justification: The change introduces 'Shares' alongside 'Securities' in subsection (iv) without defining whether they are interchangeable. Since the clause relates to a public or private offering under Section 4(a)(2) of the U.S. Securities Act, it becomes unclear whether the restriction applies equally to both 'Shares' and 'Securities', thus creating ambiguity and potentially conflicting obligations in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: Terms used in this certificate have the meanings given to them in the Agency Agreement, including Schedule A attached hereto, unless otherwise defined herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Terms used in this certificate have the meanings given to them in the Service Contract, including Schedule B attached hereto, unless otherwise defined herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Agency Agreement" to "Service Contract" and "Schedule A" to "Schedule B" in the concluding statement creates a contradiction. Schedule A is referenced within the agreement, but the changed text refers to Schedule B, even after the first paragraph of the legal agreement refers to "Schedule A". This leaves the reader in confusion whether Schedule A or B is the one used in the agreement and when either would be the correct document to reference.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Terms used in this certificate have the meanings given to them in the Service Contract, including Schedule B attached hereto, unless otherwise defined herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the referenced agreement from "Agency Agreement, including Schedule A" to "Service Contract, including Schedule B." This creates a direct contradiction because the agreement now refers to potentially different sets of terms and schedules. The contradicted text is an exact duplicate of the Changed Text. This shows there is a strong possibility of a contradictory reference somewhere else that leads to confusion of which agreement/schedule applies within the document.
